panels.title: Stats
panels.description: Duration and payload sizes for the selected call. Color thresholds: green ≤ 500ms, yellow ≤ 2000ms, red > 2000ms (durations); green ≤ 16KB, yellow ≤ 256KB, red > 256KB (payloads).

targets.rawSql: SELECT duration_ms AS duration, length(params) AS "params bytes", length(result) AS "result bytes" FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1

WITH parent AS (SELECT min(started_at) AS first_started FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE execution_id = (SELECT execution_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1)) SELECT started_at, tool_name, multiIf((SELECT first_started FROM parent) < started_at, 'wrapped', tool_name IN ('workflow_call_tool','workflow_run'), 'wrapper', 'direct') AS kind, duration_ms, toUInt64(response_bytes / 4) AS response_tokens, length(params) AS params_bytes, length(result) AS result_bytes, coalesce(if(error_code = '', NULL, error_code), 'ok') AS status, runner_id, source, session_id, execution_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1

panels.title: Sibling Calls
panels.description: Other calls that share this call's execution_id. For wrapper calls (workflow_call_tool, workflow_run) this is the inner forwarded call(s). For wrapped calls this is the parent wrapper. The current call is highlighted with `▸` and a column header note. Click any other row to inspect that call instead.

targets.rawSql: WITH this_exec AS (SELECT execution_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1) SELECT if(call_id = '$call_id', '▸', '') AS cur, started_at, tool_name, duration_ms, coalesce(if(error_code = '', NULL, error_code), 'ok') AS status, call_id, session_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE execution_id = (SELECT execution_id FROM this_exec) AND execution_id != '' ORDER BY started_at ASC

panels.title: Params
panels.description: Full untruncated params rendered in a logs panel so newlines display as visible line breaks (Grafana 11 table panels collapse \n; line-break support landed in 12.2 behind the tableNextGen flag — out of reach here). The SQL pretty-prints JSON server-side: every {/[ opens a new line and every key:value pair is on its own line. Indentation is flat (depth-aware indent isn't possible in re2 — no lookahead), so nested levels share the same 2-space indent; readability is still vastly better than a single-line blob. Non-JSON strings keep their original newlines.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT started_at AS timestamp, replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(params, '([{\[])', '\1
  '), '([}\]])', '
\1'), ',', ',
  ') AS body FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1

panels.title: Result
panels.description: Full untruncated result rendered in a logs panel so newlines display as visible line breaks (Grafana 11 table panels collapse \n; line-break support landed in 12.2 behind the tableNextGen flag — out of reach here). The SQL pretty-prints JSON server-side: every {/[ opens a new line and every key:value pair is on its own line. Indentation is flat (depth-aware indent isn't possible in re2 — no lookahead), so nested levels share the same 2-space indent; readability is still vastly better than a single-line blob. Non-JSON strings keep their original newlines.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT started_at AS timestamp, replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(replaceRegexpAll(result, '([{\[])', '\1
  '), '([}\]])', '
\1'), ',', ',
  ') AS body FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1

panels.title: Error
panels.description: Shown only when the call returned an error.

targets.rawSql: SELECT error_code, error_message FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' AND coalesce(error_message, '') != '' LIMIT 1

panels.title: Events
panels.description: Free-form events recorded for this call's execution_id. Events are not tagged with call_id today, so the panel scopes by execution_id — for direct calls (1:1 with execution_id) this is exact; for wrappers it includes the wrapper's and the wrapped child's events.

targets.rawSql: SELECT timestamp, severity, component, event, message, attributes FROM ploston.events WHERE execution_id = (SELECT execution_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE call_id = '$call_id' LIMIT 1) AND execution_id != '' ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 500

list.name: session_id
list.label: Session
list.query: SELECT session_id FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND session_id != '' GROUP BY session_id ORDER BY max(started_at) DESC

list.name: call_id
list.label: Call
list.query: SELECT call_id AS value, concat(formatDateTime(started_at, '%H:%i:%S'), '  ', tool_name, '  (', toString(duration_ms), 'ms)  [', coalesce(if(error_code = '', NULL, error_code), 'ok'), ']') AS displayValue FROM ploston.tool_calls WHERE session_id = '$session_id' AND $__timeFilter(started_at) ORDER BY started_at DESC
